senses.definition: a partial or partial turning of a part (usually a limb) so that the palm or sole faces upward or forward
examples: The surgeon noted a degree of semisupination in the patient’s forearm movements.
In the animation, semisupination of the wrist is shown during the grasp.

senses.definition: partially lying on the back with the chest facing somewhat upward
examples: The patient was placed in a semisupine position for the imaging procedure.
The instructor demonstrated a semisupine posture for the stretch.

senses.definition: a partial suspension or temporary suspension of activity or operation
examples: There was a semisuspension of services during the outage.
The project experienced semisuspension due to funding gaps.

senses.definition: having a taste that is partly sweet and partly not sweet
examples: The cake was semisweet, not as sugary as the usual recipe.
She preferred semisweet chocolate to dark chocolate.

senses.definition: partially symmetrical or possessing partial symmetry
examples: The design is semisymmetric, with mirrored elements on either side.
In mathematics, semisymmetric matrices have some but not full symmetry.

senses.definition: partially synthetic; made partly from natural sources and partly from chemical synthesis
examples: Semisynthetic antibiotics combine natural products with synthetic modifications.
The drug is semisynthetic, derived from a natural precursor and chemically altered.

senses.definition: a path or route that is half or partially created; historically used in some linguistic or botanical terms, but uncommon in everyday use
examples: The term semita appears in historical texts to describe a partial road.
In taxonomy, semita references a partial path in classification.

senses.definition: a person who semantically or partially acts as a verb or predicate in a sentence; a rare historical/linguistic term
examples: The linguist discussed the concept of a semitact in early grammars.
In ancient texts, semitacts appeared in marginal commentary.

senses.definition: a plural form in a specific scholarly context, often historical or linguistic; usage is very rare
examples: The manuscript references semitae as a class of terms.
Scholars noted several semitae within the marginal notes.

senses.definition: having a tail that is partly present or abbreviated; used in speculative or descriptive contexts
examples: The semitailed creature was described in the field notes.
In the diagram, semitailed extensions appear at the posterior end.

senses.definition: partially tall or having half of a tall stature; a rare descriptor
examples: The artist described the sculpture as semital in proportions.
A semital figure stood prominently in the display.

senses.definition: occurring in a tandem arrangement but only partly; half of a two-part pairing
examples: The gears work semitandem, providing partial synchronization.
In the display, the cars are semitandem on the track.

senses.definition: a line or curve that touches a curve at one point from the side, with partial contact
examples: In geometry, a semitangent is a line that touches the curve at a single point.

senses.definition: a common name for a fish, or a rare morphological form; usage is extremely limited
examples: The fishermen mentioned semitar in their glossary.
In some dialects, semitar refers to a local fish.

senses.definition: plural form of semitar; rare and specialized usage
examples: References to semitares appear in the historical glossary.

senses.definition: a mythical creature described as half human and half bull, or used as a playful compound in fantasy contexts
examples: In the book, the hero encounters a semitaur guarding an ancient gate.
Artists sometimes depict semitaurs in fantasy realms.

senses.definition: plural form of semitaur; used in fantasy contexts
examples: The legends spoke of semitaurs that roamed the highlands.

senses.definition: a member of one of the peoples who spoke Semitic languages, including Jews and Arabs
examples: The term semite has historical and ethnolinguistic use.
In older texts, semite is used to describe people of Semitic heritage.

senses.definition: partially technical; combines ordinary and technical elements
examples: The report uses semitechnical language that may confuse non-experts.
A semitechnical explanation was provided to aid comprehension.

senses.definition: partially abstaining from alcohol; practicing teetotalism to some extent
examples: He is semiteetotal, choosing non-alcoholic options most of the time.
The group is semiteetotal, allowing occasional drinks on special occasions.

senses.definition: relating to a slender or delicate body part; archaic or rare
examples: The poet's semitelic imagery evokes fragility.
An old botany text uses semitelic to describe a delicate structure.

senses.definition: one of the hamstring muscles on the back of the thigh
examples: The semitendinosus is stretched during the leg curl.
An injury to the semitendinosus can slow running.

senses.definition: variant spelling issue; same as semitendinosus
examples: Note the alternate spelling semitendinous in some texts.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling the semitendinosus muscle; often used as an alternate spelling
examples: The semitendinous tendon was examined during the surgery.
A semitendinous fiber was observed in the specimen.

senses.definition: having a partially rounded or even-tapered shape; used in anatomical or botanical descriptions
examples: The semiterete leaf has a broad, rounded base.
In anatomy, the semiterete segment is described as thicker at one end.

senses.definition: living partly on land and partly in water; or existing in a half-terrestrial state
examples: Some plants are semiterrestrial, thriving both on land and in shallow water.
The organism is semiterrestrial, spending part of its life cycle on land.
